nearDuplicateReview.firstStem: Pitch moves upwards, so each later note is higher than the note before it. What is this?
nearDuplicateReview.secondStem: Pitch moves downwards, so each later note is lower than the note before it. What is this?
nearDuplicateReview.review: Reviewed: the paired stems deliberately assess opposite pitch directions and differ on both the direction and resulting pitch relationship.

nearDuplicateReview.firstStem: The tempo increases, so the beat moves more quickly than before. What is this?
nearDuplicateReview.secondStem: The tempo decreases, so the beat moves less quickly than before. What is this?
nearDuplicateReview.review: Reviewed: the paired stems deliberately assess opposite tempo changes and use unambiguous increase/decrease language.

nearDuplicateReview.firstStem: A tuned percussion instrument with metal bars arranged like a keyboard and struck with hard beaters. What is this?
nearDuplicateReview.secondStem: A tuned percussion instrument with wooden bars arranged like a keyboard and struck with beaters. What is this?
nearDuplicateReview.review: Reviewed: this same-level pair deliberately tests the defining metal-bar versus wooden-bar distinction between two tuned percussion instruments.

nearDuplicateReview.firstStem: The smallest and highest-pitched standard orchestral string instrument. What is this?
nearDuplicateReview.secondStem: The smallest and highest standard orchestral woodwind instrument. What is this?
nearDuplicateReview.review: Reviewed: these cross-level questions identify the highest standard member of different orchestral families; their level banks are never mixed.

nearDuplicateReview.firstStem: The major key that has the same key signature as a given minor key. What is this?
nearDuplicateReview.secondStem: The minor key that has the same key signature as a given major key. What is this?
nearDuplicateReview.review: Reviewed: the paired stems deliberately assess the opposite relative-major and relative-minor relationships.

nearDuplicateReview.firstStem: A theme, melody or rhythm is repeated with every note value lengthened in the same proportion. What is this?
nearDuplicateReview.secondStem: A theme, melody or rhythm is repeated with every note value shortened in the same proportion. What is this?
nearDuplicateReview.review: Reviewed: this pair deliberately contrasts lengthening every note value with shortening every note value.
